---
title: "STAT 306 Project Code"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
```

# Introduction 

## Read Data

```{r}
insurance <- read.csv("insurance.csv")
summary(insurance)
```

# Analysis 

## EDA

```{r}
# Plot: Age vs charges
ggplot(insurance, aes(x = age, y = charges)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.5) +
  labs(title = "Age vs Charges",
       x = "Age",
       y = "Charges")

```

```{r}
# Plot: BMI vs charges
ggplot(insurance, aes(x = bmi, y = charges)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.5) +
  labs(title = "BMI vs Charges",
       x = "BMI",
       y = "Charges")
```

```{r}
# Plot: Boxplot of smoker v.s. charges
ggplot(insurance, aes(x = smoker, y = charges)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Smoker vs Charges",
       x = "Smoker",
       y = "Charges")

```

```{r}
# Plot: Distribution of charges (histogram)
p1 <- ggplot(insurance, aes(x = charges)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Insurance Charges",
       x = "Charges (USD)", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()

p1
ggsave("plot1_charges_distribution.png", plot = p1, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```

### Take log transform for charges

```{r}
insurance$log_charges <- log(insurance$charges)

ggplot(insurance, aes(x = log_charges)) +
  geom_histogram(fill = "#4472C4", color = "white", bins = 30) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Log Insurance Charges",
    x = "Log Charges (log USD)",
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

ggsave("log_charges_histogram.png", width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```

## Model Fitting

```{r}
# Fit an addictive model
log_charges = log(insurance$charges)
insurance$log_charges <- log(insurance$charges)
reg1=lm(log_charges~age+bmi+smoker,data=insurance)
summary(reg1)
```
